video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 26
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, May 26th: European Commission spokesperson calls Russia's demand for diplomats to leave Ukraine unacceptable; Netanyahu meets with defense officials after strike in Lebanon; Train and minibus accident in Belgium leaves four dead; Drones drop from sky in Sydney during n

video:video.video:title: Temperature records toppling as spring heat wave scorches parts of Western Europe
video:video.video:description: Unpredictable and extreme weather are becoming more frequent as Earth’s warming builds. Experts say unprecedented and deadly weather extremes that sometimes strike at abnormal times and in unusual places are putting more people in danger.

video:video.video:title: Investigation opened into deadly bus-train collision at crossing in Belgium
video:video.video:description: At least four people were killed in the crash, "including the 49-year-old driver of the bus, the 27-year-old chaperone, and two other children aged fifteen and twelve years", a prosecutor's office official said.

video:video.video:title: Aerial shots capture Hajj sites as Muslim pilgrims arrive in Mina in Saudi Arabia
video:video.video:description: Around 1.5 million Muslim pilgrims moved from Mecca to Mina on Monday as the annual Hajj pilgrimage officially began in Saudi Arabia.

video:video.video:title: At the Indy 500, tradition and community bring thousands together
video:video.video:description: Every Memorial Day weekend, Speedway, a small town on the west side of Indianapolis, becomes the center of one of the world’s largest sporting events: the Indianapolis 500.

video:video.video:title: Tensions high in Lebanon and Israel as Netnayahu threatens more strikes
video:video.video:description: Residents in Beirut’s busy Hamra neighborhood said people rushed to follow the news and monitor for possible evacuation warnings after Netanyahu announced more intensive strikes targeting Hezbollah across Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Rubio on Iran: 'either going to be a good deal or there isn't going to be one'
video:video.video:description: Rubio’s visit to India marks an effort by Washington and New Delhi to steady strained relations as the two countries seek to reinforce strategic and economic ties despite recent trade tensions.

video:video.video:title: Mexican designer turns folk art into World Cup-inspired fashion
video:video.video:description: The project, created by Mexican design collective Mexclart, features T-shirts inspired by "papel picado," the delicate cut-paper decorations commonly seen during Mexican celebrations and holidays.

video:video.video:title: A big night for BTS at the American Music Awards
video:video.video:description: BTS returned to the American Music Awards with a performance and two wins, including artist of the year. (May 26)

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo makes call for robust regulation of AI in his first manifesto
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV called Monday for robust regulation of artificial intelligence and for its developers to work for the common good rather than profit, issuing a sweeping manifesto on safeguarding humankind as the technology impacts everything from work to war.

video:video.video:title: Protesters gather at New Jersey ICE detainment facility
video:video.video:description: Federal officers and protesters clashed outside a New Jersey immigration detention center Tuesday, a day after U.S. Sen.

video:video.video:title: Worrying water level rise along Euphrates in Syria after dam discharge
video:video.video:description: Syrian authorities have urged residents living on the banks of the Euphrates river to move inland following concerns that some areas will be submerged after a rise in water levels.

video:video.video:title: Georgia marks Independence Day with military ceremony amid deep political polarization
video:video.video:description: The country became independent again in 1991 following the collapse of the Soviet Union.

video:video.video:title: Meteor seen falling beside Mayon volcano in the Philippines
video:video.video:description: The landing was caught on a Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology webcam as the Mayon volcano was experiencing a low-level eruption.

video:video.video:title: Quad foreign ministers deliver joint remarks in New Delhi
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio spoke in New Delhi on Tuesday in a joint news conference with foreign ministers from India, Australia and Japan, after holding Quad alliance talks.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV calls for robust regulation of AI
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV called on Monday for robust regulation of artificial intelligence and for its developers to work for the common good rather than profit, issuing a sweeping manifesto on safeguarding humankind as the technology impacts everything from work to war.

video:video.video:title: Europe faces stray Ukrainian drones as Kyiv targets Russian oil exports
video:video.video:description: Stray Ukrainian drones have been breaching NATO airspace as Kyiv targets Russia's oil exports. On Wednesday, Lithuanians sheltered in Vilnius after an unidentified drone crossed the border.

video:video.video:title: Deaths reported after tank implodes at Washington pulp and paper mill
video:video.video:description: The implosion of a chemical tank at a pulp and paper mill in southwestern Washington state on Tuesday killed an undisclosed number of people, local authorities and company officials confirmed.

video:video.video:title: Ferrari unveils its first fully electric car
video:video.video:description: It's the luxury auto-manufacturer's first fully electric vehicle. It has four electric motors - one for each wheel, and a maximum estimated range of 530 kilometers.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 26
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, May 26th: Iran condemns US strikes as a show of 'bad faith'; Federal court blocks Alabama’s plan for new US House map; Londoners take to the Thames to escape record heat; Saxophonist Sonny Rollins, restless jazz genius, dies.

video:video.video:title: Public defenders ask Tennessee governor to halt executions after state failed to execute inmate
video:video.video:description: Federal public defenders asked the Tennessee governor to halt all executions while a lawsuit proceeds after the state failed to execute an inmate this month.

video:video.video:title: Syria's authorities warn of rising water levels
video:video.video:description: Syria's authorities warn of rising water levels along Euphrates river after increased dam discharge, prompting authorities to urge residents living on the banks to move inland out of concerns that some areas will be submerged in water.

video:video.video:title: Group says Peru's Machu Picchu 'turning into a nightmare' for tourists
video:video.video:description: Group says Peru's Machu Picchu 'turning into a nightmare' for tourists

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV sits in Ferrari's first fully electric car
video:video.video:description: Ferrari on Tuesday presented its first-ever fully electric car to Italy’s President and Pope Leo XIV, as the rollout received a mixed response from auto critics and the markets.

video:video.video:title: Waco police capture kangaroo on the loose
video:video.video:description: The Waco Police Department said officers captured a kangaroo that was loose Monday in Waco, Texas. After a short pursuit, the kangaroo — named Bingus — was safely captured and returned to a local wildlife rescue.

video:video.video:title: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton wins GOP runoff for US Senate, ousting longtime Sen. John Cornyn
video:video.video:description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton wins GOP runoff for US Senate, ousting longtime Sen. John Cornyn

video:video.video:title: Four killed as minibus of children hit by train in one of Belgium's worst rail accidents
video:video.video:description: A train traveling at high speed hit a minibus crossing a railway in Belgium on Tuesday, killing four people, including two children, and badly injuring five other children in what officials called one of the worst rail accidents in the country's history.

video:video.video:title: US military strike on alleged drug boat in the eastern Pacific kills 1, leaves 2 survivors
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military launched another strike Tuesday on a vessel suspected of transporting drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing one man and leaving two survivors.

video:video.video:title: NASA lays out moon base plans with landers, buggies and drones at the top of the list
video:video.video:description: NASA is already ordering landers, rovers and drones for a sprawling moon base, less than two months after the Artemis II's record-breaking lunar flyaround.

video:video.video:title: Ebola emerges as latest threat to displaced families in camps in eastern DRCongo
video:video.video:description: A conflict that has been raging for three decades has left millions of people displaced, some fleeing from one camp to another since they were born.

video:video.video:title: Muslims across Asia celebrate Eid al-Adha
video:video.video:description: Muslims in Pakistan, Indian-controlled Kashmir and Indonesia gathered to pray as a way to celebrate Eid al-Adha.

video:video.video:title: Cage-match fighting coming to the White House
video:video.video:description: Yet another White House construction project is underway, though this one is meant to be only temporary.

video:video.video:title: Rescue efforts continue for seven people trapped inside flooded cave in Laos
video:video.video:description: The villagers entered the cave in Xaisomboun province on May 19, but heavy rain triggered flash flooding that blocked the exit, according to Lao and Thai rescue teams involved in the operation.

video:video.video:title: Rare public trial opens in Paris child abuse case as parents seek national wake-up call
video:video.video:description: Parents’ groups in France on Tuesday called for more attention to long-ignored child abuse scandals as a rare public trial opened of a school assistant accused of sexually assaulting nine small children in Paris.

video:video.video:title: Impact of war in Iran and closure of Hormuz Strait felt acutely in Afghanistan
video:video.video:description: When landlocked Afghanistan and neighboring Pakistan started fighting late last year and their border crossings closed, Afghans turned to their western neighbor, Iran, for an alternate to Pakistan’s major shipping hub of Karachi.

video:video.video:title: Central Seoul overpass collapses during demolition work
video:video.video:description: At least three people were killed and three others were injured on Tuesday when a decades-old overpass undergoing demolition partially collapsed in the South Korean capital.

video:video.video:title: 'We've come up short' Senator John Cornyn said after losing the Texas primary election
video:video.video:description: Sen. John Cornyn conceded defeat in the Texas Senate primary runoff after Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton secured victory in a massively expensive race.

video:video.video:title: Exceptionally early heat wave shatters records and brings deaths in Europe
video:video.video:description: Heatwave is sweeping across Europe as the United Kingdom smashed a century-old temperature record for the second time in 24 hours on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: K-pop stars IDID share best advice
video:video.video:description: Members of Kpop outfit IDID reveal the best advice they have received from fellow musicians. (May 27)

video:video.video:title: Biden sues Justice Department to stop release of interview audio tied to special counsel probe
video:video.video:description: Joe Biden sued the Justice Department to block the release of audio recordings and transcripts of the former president's interview with a ghostwriter that were obtained by the special counsel who investigated his handling of classified documents.

video:video.video:title: Suicide deaths of ICE detainees surge to new high as experts see failures in care
video:video.video:description: An Associated Press investigation found that at least 10 detainees, all men, have died by suicide since President Donald Trump took office in January 2025, a pace that far exceeds the growth in the detainee population.

video:video.video:title: Packers coach Matt LaFleur on Josh Jacobs
video:video.video:description: Green Bay Packers running back Josh Jacobs was released from a Wisconsin jail while authorities further investigate allegations of domestic abuse.

video:video.video:title: Rubio insists no Ebola cases will be allowed in the US
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Wednesday the U.S. “cannot and will not allow” Ebola cases into the country as officials work to contain the outbreak in Africa.

video:video.video:title: Gaza displaced mark Eid al-Adha amid deprivation in war-wrecked territory
video:video.video:description: Eid al-Adha is normally one of the most important Islamic holidays, marked by family gatherings, large meals and the ritual slaughter of livestock, with meat shared among relatives and those in need.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia ramps up Taiwan investment as Huang calls island the 'epicenter' of AI revolution
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said Wednesday the tech giant will ramp up its annual spending with Taiwanese supply chain partners to $150 billion, highlighting the island's expanding role at the heart of the global artificial intelligence boom.

video:video.video:title: Trump talks up reflecting pool remodel for nine minutes during Cabinet meeting amid Iran war
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump offered painstaking details on his Reflecting Pool project in a lengthy nine-minute tangent during a Cabinet meeting, with a wide-ranging discussion on the war in Iran, Ebola patients, and Trump accounts.

video:video.video:title: 'We're dead people': Palestinians in Gaza mark Eid but says there is 'no joy'
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of Palestinians gathered for Eid prayers on Wednesday while reflecting on the joy the holiday once held, and all the war has taken from them.

video:video.video:title: Israel allows rare access to Yellow Line between Gaza area it controls and rest of territory
video:video.video:description: At an outpost in the central Gaza Strip, Israeli soldiers are enforcing what is known as the "yellow line”, the boundary between Israeli and Hamas-controlled areas of Gaza since the sides agreed to a ceasefire last October.

video:video.video:title: Muslim pilgrims perform the sacred rituals of the hajj pilgrimage at Mount Arafat
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Muslim pilgrims climbed the rocky hill of Al Rahmah on Mount Arafat in Saudi Arabia to mark the spiritual climax of the Hajj pilgrimage.

video:video.video:title: Pilgrims perform Hajj rituals in Saudi Arabia as Eid al-Adha celebrations start
video:video.video:description: Pilgrims in Saudi Arabia threw pebbles at a large pillar in a symbolic ritual during the final days of Hajj. This act is seen as a symbolic stoning of the devil.

video:video.video:title: Green card hopefuls could be forced to apply from abroad under Trump policy
video:video.video:description: Foreigners living legally in the U.S. who want a green card may soon have to leave the country and apply from abroad under a new Trump administration policy that has sparked confusion and concern among immigration lawyers and advocacy groups.

video:video.video:title: Robotics team looks at its creation as a new species, not copy of humans or animals
video:video.video:description: Argus looks more like a virus than a robot, and that's the point. The team at Duke University's General Robotics Lab says they're out to create a new species, not just more copies of humans, dogs or birds.

video:video.video:title: Widespread devastation in Odesa as daytime Russia strike injures several
video:video.video:description: Two children and eight others are in hospital after a Russian daytime strike on the Ukrainian port city of Odesa Wednesday, according to the region's military administration head Oleh Kiper.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 27
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, May 27th: Trump convenes Cabinet as talks to end Iran war remain fluid; Rubio insists no Ebola cases will enter US; Paris heatwave prompts cooling measures at Eiffel Tower; Thousands in Lagos gather for start of Eid al-Kabir.

video:video.video:title: The Atlanta Stadium gets a makeover ahead of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: Mercedes-Benz Stadium, renamed Atlanta Stadium in accordance with FIFA regulations, is getting a makeover ahead of the World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Paxton dominates Cornyn in Texas US Senate runoff, the latest sign of Trump’s hold on GOP
video:video.video:description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton won the Republican nomination for U.S. Senate on Tuesday, easily defeating four-term Sen.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he opposes Russia or China retrieving Iran’s highly enriched uranium stockpile
video:video.video:description: Nuclear analysts have said they could be a potential acceptable third party to the Iranian Republic to take possession of the enriched uranium, which could be used to make a nuclear weapon, as part of a potential deal with the U.S. to end the war.

video:video.video:title: 'Those countries owe it to us:' Trump ties Iran deal to Gulf states joining Abraham Accords
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump suggested the U.S. may not move forward with an Iran deal unless more Gulf nations join the Abraham Accords, saying some countries “owe that to us" and should normalize ties with Israel as negotiations with Tehran continue.

video:video.video:title: Scripps National Spelling Bee Returns to DC for 98th Edition
video:video.video:description: The best young spellers in the English language are competing at the Scripps National Spelling Bee this week. (AP Video by Ben Nuckols)

video:video.video:title: Congressmen Goldman and Nadler enter ICE facility in New Jersey amid protests
video:video.video:description: Rep. Daniel Goldman and Rep. Jerrold Nadler were escorted into detention facility amid concerns about conditions inside. (AP Video by Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:title: Funeral for senior Hamas commander killed in Israel strike with wife and children
video:video.video:description: Hamas confirmed in a statement on Wednesday the killing of Odeh, a senior commander, who they said was killed on Tuesday along with his wife and two of his children in an Israeli airstrike that targeted a residential building in Gaza City.

video:video.video:title: Deaths reported after tank implodes at Washington pulp and paper mill
video:video.video:description: Authorities say an undisclosed number of people have been killed and or remained missing after the implosion of a chemical tank at a pulp and paper mill in southwestern Washington state on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: First group of Ghanaians leaves South Africa under voluntary repatriation program
video:video.video:description: More than 800 Ghanaians registered with the Ghana High Commission in Pretoria for evacuation following weeks of protests and rising fears among foreign nationals.

video:video.video:title: Chemical tank implosion in Washington state kills 1 and leaves 9 missing
video:video.video:description: The severity of the injuries ranged from minor to critical, with some suffering burns or inhalation injuries, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Muslims celebrate Eid al-Adha across the world
video:video.video:description: A joyous occasion celebrated by Muslims around the world, Eid al-Adha marks Prophet Ibrahim’s test of faith and his willingness to sacrifice his son in submission to God.

video:video.video:title: 5 of the 7 missing villagers trapped in flooded Laos cave have been found alive
video:video.video:description: Five villagers stuck in a flooded cave in central Laos for more than a week were found alive, rescuers said Wednesday, but two others are missing.

video:video.video:title: K-pop band IDID show 'our natural selves' on new album
video:video.video:description: K-pop band IDID has released a new album "FLY!" which they say captures their "natural selves" throughout (May 27).

video:video.video:title: Waco police capture kangaroo on the loose in Texas
video:video.video:description: The Waco Police Department said officers captured a kangaroo that was loose Monday in Waco, Texas. After a short pursuit, the kangaroo, named Bingus, was safely captured and returned to a local wildlife rescue.

video:video.video:title: Ebola Is the latest threat to displaced families in camps in eastern DRC
video:video.video:description: Internally displaced people in the conflict-ridden east of the Democratic Republic of Congo are now confronting another threat: Ebola.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers celebrate as they find missing villagers in flooded Laos cave
video:video.video:description: The villagers entered the cave in Xaisomboun province on May 19, but heavy rain triggered flash flooding that blocked the exit and trapped seven people, according to Lao and Thai rescue teams involved in the operation.

video:video.video:title: Caregiving robot helps disabled American couple without home support
video:video.video:description: Caregiving robots are helping people living with disabilities with daily tasks from exercising to taking medication.

video:video.video:title: Tiny Curaçao gets ready for its first World Cup
video:video.video:description: For the first time, Curaçao is competing in the most-watched sporting show on Earth. The island is the smallest country by population to ever qualify for the World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz warned protesters that if they don't agree to talk, they will be punished by the law
video:video.video:description: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz warned protesters that if they don't agree to talk, they will be punished by the law.

video:video.video:title: Penélope Cruz: 'The younger generation are victims of social media'
video:video.video:description: Penelope Cruz thinks social media has made being a youth today more difficult than when she was growing up.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy says he wrote Trump and Congress a letter asking for more US air defense help
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy confirmed Wednesday that he had written a letter to US President Donald Trump and Congress asking for more US-made air defense ammunition.

video:video.video:title: History already made as tiny Curaçao heads to World Cup
video:video.video:description: Curaçao has already crafted a story like none other in World Cup history. A tiny island country of about 156,000 residents is now the smallest, both in terms of population and land mass, to make it to soccer’s biggest stage.

video:video.video:title: Catherine Zeta-Jones taps into her motherly instincts to play Morticia Addams
video:video.video:description: Catherine Zeta-Jones says she was able to tap into her own motherly instincts to play Morticia Addams in the Netflix series "Wednesday."

video:video.video:title: Cristian Mungiu says 'Fjord' aims to entertain while encouraging tolerance
video:video.video:description: Cristian Mungiu says his Palme d’Or-winning film "Fjord" aims to entertain audiences while encouraging tolerance.

video:video.video:title: 9 missing after Washington paper mill tank rupture and officials say there's no hope of survivors
video:video.video:description: A second person who was injured when a large chemical tank ruptured at a Washington state paper mill has died, authorities said Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Sir Derek Jacobi is 87, but retirement isn't in the plans
video:video.video:description: Award-winning actor Sir Derek Jacobi is 87 years old, but he doesn't plan on retiring any time soon.

video:video.video:title: J Lo on the resurgence of her hit 'On The Floor'
video:video.video:description: Jennifer Lopez reacts to the resurgence of her 2011 hit "On the Floor." Now she stars in the Netflix rom-com "Office Romance."

video:video.video:title: Uganda closes border with DRCongo as cases of rare Ebola type surge
video:video.video:description: Ugandan authorities on Wednesday ordered the closure of the border with the Democratic Republic of Congo as suspected cases of a rare type of Ebola surge to near 1,000 in its neighbor and as other variants emerge at home.

video:video.video:title: Tehran residents welcome easing of internet shutdown imposed after start of war
video:video.video:description: Iranian authorities have eased a monthslong internet shutdown that they cast as a wartime necessity, but that has cost the country's economy an estimated $30-$40 million a day.

video:video.video:title: Scripps National Spelling Bee returns
video:video.video:description: The Scripps National Spelling Bee is back this week. The bee began in 1925, with this year marking its 98th edition.

video:video.video:title: Spain's Sanchez says his ruling party is cooperating with police after raid on headquarters
video:video.video:description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said Wednesday that his party is cooperating with police, amid an ongoing investigation into possible financial wrongdoing.

video:video.video:title: Penpa Tsering sworn in for second term at head of Tibet’s government-in-exile
video:video.video:description: Tsering, 58, has led the exile government based in Dharamshala, India, since 2021. He secured another five-year term in elections held in February among Tibetans living in India and overseas.

video:video.video:title: An early heatwave in Paris prompted cooling measures at the Eiffel Tower as temperatures soared
video:video.video:description: A rise in temperatures brought highs of around 33 degrees Celsius (91 F) to Paris this week as an early heatwave swept across France creating sweltering conditions for tourists and locals alike.

video:video.video:title: James Talarico kicks off general election by targeting Ken Paxton's scandals
video:video.video:description: Texas Democrat James Talarico launched his U.S. Senate campaign Wednesday by framing Republican opponent and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton as part of the corrupt elite more than promoting his own policy agenda.

video:video.video:title: Divers find 5 villagers alive in a flooded cave in Laos after more than a week trapped in darkness
video:video.video:description: Five villagers who became trapped in a flooded cave in central Laos over a week ago have been found alive by divers who discovered them sitting on a rock in the darkness, rescuers said Wednesday, but two others are still missing.

video:video.video:title: Think it's warm now? The next five years look even record-breaking hotter, UN weather agency warns
video:video.video:description: A new World Meteorological Organization report gives a three-out-of-four chance that the next five years will average more than 1.5 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial temperatures.

video:video.video:title: US military strikes another alleged drug boat in the eastern Pacific, killing 2
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military on Wednesday struck another vessel suspected of transporting drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing two men. U.S.

video:video.video:title: Asylum seekers deported by US detained in Equatorial Guinea hotel
video:video.video:description: Under an opaque $7.5 million deal with the Trump administration, Equatorial Guinea’s all-powerful president has turned a hotel owned by his family into a prison for asylum seekers deported from the United States.

video:video.video:title: Jennifer Lopez arrives at the Los Angeles Latino International Film Festival
video:video.video:description: Jennifer Lopez arrives at the Los Angeles Latino International Film Festival (LALIFF) where she received the inaugural Adelante Award for Industry. (May 27)

video:video.video:title: Under President Milei's austerity, disabled Argentines risk losing essential services
video:video.video:description: In recent months, the Argentine government has frozen payments to organizations providing therapeutic and educational services to people with disabilities.

video:video.video:title: PM Netanyahu announces 'deepening' of Israeli military operation in Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that he had ordered a "deepening" of his country's military operation in Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Andrew Scott takes on the 'gripping story' of the meteorologist who saved D-Day
video:video.video:description: D-Day was supposed to happen on June 5, 1944, but ultimately took place the next day because of a Scottish meteorologist played by Andrew Scott in new drama "Pressure."

video:video.video:title: Fire at school for girls in central Kenya causes unknown number of deaths
video:video.video:description: Authorities are trying to account for all the students at Utumishi Girls School in the Gilgil area on Thursday after a fire broke out.
